# Zuke

> Code-first, strongly-typed build automation for Deno/TypeScript. Define a build by extending `Build`; declare targets with the `target()` fluent builder, wiring dependencies as `this.<field>` references (not strings) for compile-time safety. Every external tool has a typed `*Tasks` wrapper in a settings-lambda style ‚Äî never shell out by hand.

## Get the exact API ‚Äî do not guess

- Whole typed surface of every package, in one file: [llms-full.txt](./llms-full.txt)
- Scaffold/install: `deno run -A jsr:@zuke/cli setup`
- A single package's API on the command line: `deno doc jsr:@zuke/<package>`

## Example

```ts
import { Build, run, target } from "jsr:@zuke/core";
import { DenoTasks } from "jsr:@zuke/deno";

class CI extends Build {
  lint = target().executes(() => DenoTasks.lint());
  test = target().dependsOn(this.lint)
    .executes(() => DenoTasks.test((s) => s.allowAll()));
}

await run(CI);
```

## CLI

Run a build with the `zuke` command ‚Äî `deno run -A zuke.ts <target>` (or
the `./zuke` launcher). The CLI is self-describing:

```sh
zuke <target> [--skip <dep>] [--parallel[=N]]    # run a target and its deps
zuke --list [--json]                             # list targets (JSON: full surface)
zuke graph [--output=html]                       # dependency graph
zuke generate-ci [--check]                       # write declared CI files
zuke completions <print|install> <bash|zsh|fish> # shell completion
```

`zuke --help` prints the usage grammar plus the build's live targets and
parameters; `zuke --list --json` emits the whole surface for tools.

Reserved commands:

- `graph` ‚Äî Show the dependency graph
- `generate-ci` ‚Äî Write declared CI configuration files
- `completions` ‚Äî Print a shell-completion script
- `mcp` ‚Äî Run an MCP server over the build (for AI agents)
- `resume` ‚Äî Resume a suspended run (or --check all suspended runs)
- `runs` ‚Äî List, show, or prune persisted run records
- `cancel` ‚Äî Cancel a run and run its compensations
- `register` ‚Äî Register this build in the build registry
- `doc` ‚Äî Print a package's API docs (deno doc), isolated from the repo

Option flags:

- `--list` ‚Äî List all targets with descriptions
- `--json` ‚Äî Print the build surface (commands, flags, targets) as JSON
- `--skip` ‚Äî Skip the named dependency
- `--parallel` ‚Äî Run independent targets concurrently
- `--no-cache` ‚Äî Ignore the incremental cache
- `--no-remote-cache` ‚Äî Use the local cache only; skip the remote cache store
- `--affected` ‚Äî Run only targets affected by changes since a git base
- `--dry-run` ‚Äî Print the plan without running targets
- `--state` ‚Äî Persist durable run state to .zuke/runs
- `--actor` ‚Äî Attribute the run to <name> in its state record
- `--output` ‚Äî Graph output format: text or html
- `--no-open` ‚Äî With --output=html, do not open a browser
- `--check` ‚Äî With generate-ci verify files; with resume re-check suspended runs
- `--signal` ‚Äî With resume, deliver a named external signal
- `--data` ‚Äî With resume --signal, the signal's JSON payload
- `--force-graph` ‚Äî With resume, continue even if the build graph changed
- `--status` ‚Äî With runs list, keep only runs with this status
- `--target` ‚Äî With runs list, keep only runs whose graph has this target
- `--since` ‚Äî With runs list, keep only runs created at/after this time
- `--limit` ‚Äî With runs list, return at most this many runs (newest)
- `--counts` ‚Äî With runs list, print aggregate counts (total + per status)
- `--keep` ‚Äî With runs prune, keep runs newer than this age (e.g. 90d)
- `--keep-last` ‚Äî With runs prune, always keep the newest N terminal runs
- `--allow-run` ‚Äî With mcp, let agents run targets (optional =<glob-list> allow-list)
- `--protect` ‚Äî With mcp, require an operator token to run these targets
- `--allowed-origin` ‚Äî With mcp --http, an extra allowed Origin (repeatable; loopback-only otherwise)
- `--confirm-destructive` ‚Äî With mcp, require confirm:true before a destructive run
- `--registry` ‚Äî With mcp, serve the build registry (dynamic pipeline discovery)
- `--max-concurrent-runs` ‚Äî With mcp --registry, cap concurrent run-tool spawns (default 4)
- `--http` ‚Äî With mcp, serve over HTTP on <host:port> instead of stdio
- `--help` ‚Äî Show usage

Full reference: [docs/cli.md](./docs/cli.md).

## Packages

- [@zuke/core](https://jsr.io/@zuke/core) ‚Äî a code-first, strongly-typed build automation system for Deno
- [@zuke/deno](https://jsr.io/@zuke/deno) ‚Äî typed `DenoTasks` wrappers for the `deno` CLI, for use in
- [@zuke/docs](https://jsr.io/@zuke/docs) ‚Äî typed tasks that turn already-generated API documentation into
- [@zuke/npm](https://jsr.io/@zuke/npm) ‚Äî typed `NpmTasks` wrappers for the `npm` CLI, for use in Zuke
- [@zuke/npx](https://jsr.io/@zuke/npx) ‚Äî typed `NpxTasks` wrappers for the `npx` package runner, for use
- [@zuke/bun](https://jsr.io/@zuke/bun) ‚Äî typed `BunTasks` wrappers for the `bun` CLI, for use in Zuke
- [@zuke/pnpm](https://jsr.io/@zuke/pnpm) ‚Äî typed `PnpmTasks` wrappers for the `pnpm` CLI, for use in Zuke
- [@zuke/yarn](https://jsr.io/@zuke/yarn) ‚Äî typed `YarnTasks` wrappers for the `yarn` CLI, for use in Zuke
- [@zuke/cmd](https://jsr.io/@zuke/cmd) ‚Äî generic command execution for Zuke builds: the fallback for
- [@zuke/console](https://jsr.io/@zuke/console) ‚Äî task-shaped console output for Zuke builds, so a build never
- [@zuke/cli](https://jsr.io/@zuke/cli) ‚Äî the `zuke` command. Install it globally with
- [@zuke/docker](https://jsr.io/@zuke/docker) ‚Äî typed `docker` CLI task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/docker-compose](https://jsr.io/@zuke/docker-compose) ‚Äî typed Docker Compose task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/kubectl](https://jsr.io/@zuke/kubectl) ‚Äî typed `kubectl` CLI task wrappers for Zuke builds, for
- [@zuke/helm](https://jsr.io/@zuke/helm) ‚Äî typed `HelmTasks` wrappers for the Helm (https://helm.sh) CLI,
- [@zuke/kustomize](https://jsr.io/@zuke/kustomize) ‚Äî typed `KustomizeTasks` wrappers for the
- [@zuke/oxlint](https://jsr.io/@zuke/oxlint) ‚Äî typed `oxlint` task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/eslint](https://jsr.io/@zuke/eslint) ‚Äî typed `eslint` task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/cspell](https://jsr.io/@zuke/cspell) ‚Äî typed `cspell` task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/jest](https://jsr.io/@zuke/jest) ‚Äî typed `jest` task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/vitest](https://jsr.io/@zuke/vitest) ‚Äî typed `vitest` task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/playwright](https://jsr.io/@zuke/playwright) ‚Äî typed `PlaywrightTasks` wrappers for the Playwright CLI,
- [@zuke/cypress](https://jsr.io/@zuke/cypress) ‚Äî typed `CypressTasks` wrappers for the
- [@zuke/biome](https://jsr.io/@zuke/biome) ‚Äî typed `BiomeTasks` wrappers for the Biome (https://biomejs.dev)
- [@zuke/knip](https://jsr.io/@zuke/knip) ‚Äî a typed `KnipTasks` wrapper for the Knip (https://knip.dev)
- [@zuke/dpdm](https://jsr.io/@zuke/dpdm) ‚Äî a typed `DpdmTasks` wrapper for the
- [@zuke/jsr](https://jsr.io/@zuke/jsr) ‚Äî tools for the JSR (https://jsr.io) registry in Zuke builds:
- [@zuke/vite](https://jsr.io/@zuke/vite) ‚Äî typed `ViteTasks` wrappers for the Vite (https://vitejs.dev)
- [@zuke/tsup](https://jsr.io/@zuke/tsup) ‚Äî a typed `TsupTasks` wrapper for the
- [@zuke/turbo](https://jsr.io/@zuke/turbo) ‚Äî typed `TurboTasks` wrappers for the
- [@zuke/nx](https://jsr.io/@zuke/nx) ‚Äî typed `NxTasks` wrappers for the Nx (https://nx.dev) CLI, for
- [@zuke/tsx](https://jsr.io/@zuke/tsx) ‚Äî typed `tsx` task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/tsgo](https://jsr.io/@zuke/tsgo) ‚Äî typed `tsgo` task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/tsc](https://jsr.io/@zuke/tsc) ‚Äî typed `tsc` task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/tsc-alias](https://jsr.io/@zuke/tsc-alias) ‚Äî typed `tsc-alias` task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/tsdown](https://jsr.io/@zuke/tsdown) ‚Äî a typed `TsdownTasks` wrapper for the
- [@zuke/nest](https://jsr.io/@zuke/nest) ‚Äî typed NestJS CLI (`nest`) task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/openapi-ts](https://jsr.io/@zuke/openapi-ts) ‚Äî typed `openapi-ts` task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/orval](https://jsr.io/@zuke/orval) ‚Äî typed `orval` task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/husky](https://jsr.io/@zuke/husky) ‚Äî typed `husky` task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/node](https://jsr.io/@zuke/node) ‚Äî typed Node.js task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/dprint](https://jsr.io/@zuke/dprint) ‚Äî typed `dprint` task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/gcloud](https://jsr.io/@zuke/gcloud) ‚Äî typed Google Cloud tooling for Zuke builds: the `gcloud`
- [@zuke/git](https://jsr.io/@zuke/git) ‚Äî typed `git` task wrappers for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/gh](https://jsr.io/@zuke/gh) ‚Äî typed GitHub tooling for Zuke builds: the `gh` (GitHub CLI) task
- [@zuke/codecov](https://jsr.io/@zuke/codecov) ‚Äî a typed Codecov CLI (`codecovcli`) task wrapper for Zuke
- [@zuke/claude](https://jsr.io/@zuke/claude) ‚Äî a typed Claude Code (https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code)
- [@zuke/codex](https://jsr.io/@zuke/codex) ‚Äî a typed OpenAI Codex (https://developers.openai.com/codex/cli)
- [@zuke/gemini](https://jsr.io/@zuke/gemini) ‚Äî a typed Gemini CLI (https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli)
- [@zuke/terraform](https://jsr.io/@zuke/terraform) ‚Äî typed `TerraformTasks` wrappers for the `terraform` CLI,
- [@zuke/tofu](https://jsr.io/@zuke/tofu) ‚Äî typed `TofuTasks` wrappers for the OpenTofu CLI (`tofu`), for
- [@zuke/release-please](https://jsr.io/@zuke/release-please) ‚Äî typed `ReleasePleaseTasks` wrappers for the
- [@zuke/security](https://jsr.io/@zuke/security) ‚Äî typed task wrappers for free, open-source security scanners
- [@zuke/ai](https://jsr.io/@zuke/ai) ‚Äî AI-powered code review for Zuke builds
- [@zuke/otel](https://jsr.io/@zuke/otel) ‚Äî an OpenTelemetry (OTLP/HTTP JSON) export plugin for Zuke
